data.text: I’ll use the `frontend-design` skill for the Svelte UI work, then read the existing efficacy page and e2e spec before editing.
I found this is a mixed CLI/web repo. Next I’m locating the Svelte app, the existing efficacy route, and the Playwright spec so the implementation matches the test contract instead of guessing.

The worktree already has unrelated dirty skill files, so I’ll avoid touching those and stage only the route/spec/snapshot files this bead needs. I’m reading the US-096 spec and existing test fixtures now.

The schema already has `efficacyRows`, `efficacyAttempts`, `comparisons`, and `comparisonDispatch`; filtering is client-side because the GraphQL field currently has no filter arguments. I’m adding a project efficacy route that preserves filter state in the URL, uses the existing GraphQL client, and keeps compare results locally after dispatch so the result link appears immediately.
The route and nav entry are in place. I’m formatting the touched Svelte/TS files, then I’ll run the targeted Playwright spec to catch behavior mismatches.
Formatting could not run because the frontend dependencies are not installed in this worktree. I’m checking dependency state before deciding whether to install or use the already available tooling.

There’s no `node_modules` under the frontend, so the local formatter/check runner cannot resolve project plugins. I’m installing the frontend dependencies from the existing `bun.lock`; I’ll keep generated dependency directories untracked.
The new page is formatted. I’m running Svelte’s type/check pass next; after that I’ll run only `e2e/efficacy.spec.ts` as required by the bead.
Type checking is clean for the new page; the remaining warnings are pre-existing files. I’m running the bead’s targeted Playwright spec now.

The targeted spec didn’t start because Playwright’s configured port `4173` is occupied. I’m checking the test server config and the process on that port so I can rerun without changing the project setup unnecessarily.

Several parallel execution worktrees are currently using the shared Playwright preview port. I’ll wait briefly for that server to free up, then rerun the efficacy spec against the normal project config.

The first four efficacy tests are green. The last one failed because each attempt row had two links matching the test regex; I’m tightening the evidence link label so the bead click-through and evidence bundle are distinct accessible links.

`e2e/efficacy.spec.ts` is now 5/5 green. The bead also asks for screenshots, so I’m adding the efficacy page to the visual snapshot suite and seeding the static-preview GraphQL fallback with representative efficacy rows before updating only the new efficacy snapshots.
Another Playwright run claimed the shared preview port between checks. I’m waiting for port `4173` again, then I’ll generate only the efficacy visual snapshots.

The efficacy visual snapshots were generated for light and dark modes. I’m doing the final verification pass now: targeted e2e again after snapshot/fallback edits, type check, then I’ll stage only the bead files and commit.

The combined verification hit a stale preview server on the first Playwright test, visible from the error snapshot: the nav did not include the new Efficacy route. I’m rerunning the targeted specs separately after the port clears so the result is from this worktree’s build.

The Playwright web server is now crashing during this worktree’s preview run after serving an old fallback once. I’m checking the frontend build/preview directly so I can separate application failures from the shared Playwright harness instability.

The app build itself is clean, but Playwright’s built-in webServer is unreliable here because multiple execution worktrees contend for the fixed port. I’m verifying against this worktree’s already-built preview on a private port with a temporary Playwright config, then I’ll restore generated build output.

Verification is complete on a private preview port: `efficacy.spec.ts` passed 5/5 and the two efficacy screenshot tests passed. I’m staging only the efficacy route, nav/client support, screenshot spec, and new snapshot PNGs now; the pre-existing dirty skill files are being left untouched.

Implemented and committed:

`0c95032e feat: add efficacy comparison view [ddx-b5115410]`

Verified:
- `bun run check` passed
- `e2e/efficacy.spec.ts` passed 5/5
- Efficacy light/dark screenshot specs passed and snapshots are committed

Note: the shared Playwright port was contended by other worktrees, so final e2e verification ran against this worktree’s preview on a private port. Unrelated pre-existing dirty skill files were left untouched.
